prediction.reasoning: Based on AMZN's historical volatility of approximately 30-35% annually, I estimated a 22-day standard deviation of around 4.3%. The point estimate assumes no directional bias over the short 22-day period. Confidence intervals are constructed assuming log-normal price distribution, with the 50% CI representing ±0.67 standard deviations, 80% CI representing ±1.28 standard deviations, and 90% CI representing ±1.65 standard deviations from the current price.
